steps.title: Identify the independent clauses
steps.content:
Clause 1: “The sand at Spiaggia Rosa Beach in Italy is a more unusual shade”
Clause 2: “deposits of crushed coral and other organic matter lend the sand a unique pink hue”

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule for independent clauses with terminal elements
steps.content: Two independent clauses are properly separated by a semicolon. When the first clause ends with a contrasting word like “though,” a comma precedes it and a semicolon follows it. This comma-though-semicolon pattern correctly shows the relationship between the ideas while maintaining proper clause boundaries. Options A and B position “though” incorrectly within the clause structure, while option C fails to use a comma and places “though” at the start of the second clause. That matches choice D — D is correct.
